summary: [deterministic] all deterministic checks passed for engineering-vllm-inference-blueprint | [judge] [fallback from codex] All nine stated success criteria are met. cargo check/clippy/fmt/test all pass clean (32 tests, zero warnings). Config-driven vLLM endpoint via OnceLock, stderr draining via tokio BufReader, semaphore concurrency limiting, and CI workflow are all correctly implemented. Prior round's six findings (identity check, overcharge, error propagation, streaming rejection, billing cost tests, tampered signature tests) are resolved. Three low-severity observations remain but are architectural design choices, not code defects blocking deployment.

findings.title: spend_auth.amount parsed as u64 with silent fallback to 0
findings.body: server.rs:232 parses the SpendAuth amount (which is U256 on-chain) as u64 via unwrap_or(0). If the authorized amount exceeds u64::MAX, charge_amount becomes 0, the `if charge_amount > 0` guard skips billing entirely, and inference is served without payment. The amount was already validated as U256 in verify_spend_auth_signature, but the u64 truncation here creates a discrepancy.

findings.title: reqwest::Client allocated per on-chain job invocation
findings.body: lib.rs:85 creates a new reqwest::Client on every run_inference call. Each instantiation creates a new connection pool and TLS session cache. Under load from on-chain job submissions, this adds unnecessary overhead. The client should be stored in a static or passed via context. This is the on-chain job path only (not the HTTP proxy path, which correctly reuses the client in VllmProcess).

findings.title: Billing is opt-in at HTTP layer — no spend_auth means free inference
findings.body: chat_completions serves inference regardless of whether spend_auth is present (line 190: `if let Some`). This is likely intentional for development/testing, but in production an operator would want to require billing. If this is by design, no action needed; if not, the handler should reject requests without spend_auth when billing is configured.
findings.evidence: server.rs:190 — `if let Some(ref spend_auth) = req.spend_auth {` — absence of spend_auth is silently accepted
